title: elymus macrolepis
intro: Elymus macrolepis, also known as big-spurred grass or tall wheat grass, is a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of Asia, particularly in China, Nepal, and Tibet. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and promoting digestive health. Elymus macrolepis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional Chinese medicine to support overall well-being.
